content.problemStatement:
You and your friend are playing a game with hoops. There are NNN hoops (where NNN is odd) in a row. You jump into hoop 111, and your friend jumps into hoop NNN. Then you jump into hoop 222, and after that, your friend jumps into hoop N−1N-1N−1, and so on.

The process ends when someone cannot make the next jump because the hoop is occupied by the other person. Find the last hoop that will be jumped into.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single odd integer N (1 ≤ N < 2·10^5).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the last hoop that will be jumped into.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: N=1, only hoop 1 exists, so answer is 1.\nTest case 2: N=3, hoops are 1,2,3. You jump 1, friend jumps 3, you jump 2 (middle). The friend cannot move further, so answer is 2.

solution.approach: Both players fill the hoops from the ends towards the centre, alternating turns, starting with you at hoop 1. Since N is odd, there is a unique middle hoop m = (N+1)/2. You will be the first to reach this middle hoop because you move on the 1st, 3rd, 5th, ... turns, while the friend moves on the 2nd, 4th, ... turns. After you occupy the middle hoop, the friend cannot move any further. Therefore the answer for each test case is simply (N+1)/2.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in O(1) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
